dimensions.rationale: The immutable receipt matches the requested candidate and tree, records LOCAL_SYNTHETIC_ONLY scope, zero remote writes, and no protected execution or sensitive data.

The exact receipt contains 15 unique zero-exit candidate-bound commands with tool and output digests, while the v2 provenance validator preserves and hash-binds all three ordered attempts for both model families.

The evidence consistently limits its claims to retained local synthetic execution, explicitly withholds production and protected conclusions, and acknowledges that platform task identity requires the Codex audit log.
No external mutation is evidenced, the ephemeral validation image was removed, and the immutable Git candidate remains the local rollback boundary.

findings.finding: Candidate, request, reply, model-family, task-ID, receipt, and distinct-execution identities are mechanically bound across the preserved three-attempt histories.
findings.resolution_ref: The v2 schema and assurance validator require exact ordered attempts, exact model/task/path identities, six distinct platform task IDs, shared candidate/request/receipt bindings, and distinct final replies.

findings.finding: Local PASS evidence is bound to the exact candidate and preserves the complete required command set, zero exit codes, tool versions, raw metadata, sanitized outputs, and verified sanitized-output digests.
findings.resolution_ref: The final receipt hash is exact and its 15 unique commands match the validator's closed command-ID set; schema and validator checks reject stale targets, failed commands, missing commands, digest drift, local-path leakage, and sensitive output.

findings.finding: Sensitive writes now require prospective capacity demand and reserve cumulative projected demand before invoking effectful callbacks.
findings.resolution_ref: CapacityPolicy projects repository, LFS, object, and release-asset demand; OperationalGate rejects absent or limit-crossing projections and retains reservations after uncertain remote failures.

findings.finding: Post-review drift protection now covers every workflow plus the production, Stage 6/7, dependency, runtime, test, evidence-capture, secret-scan, and assurance-validation surfaces material to this review.
findings.resolution_ref: The protected prefix set includes all .github/workflows paths and capture_candidate_gates.py, and real temporary-Git regression cases prove production-workflow and capture-tool drift block closure while documentation-only drift remains permitted.

limitations: This static re-review did not execute tests, validators, workflows, network calls, credentials, protected Oracles, Gmail, private-repository operations, deployment, or publication.
The receipt proves only local synthetic execution; repository hashes and task IDs are not signed third-party attestations and require the retained Codex thread audit log for platform identity.
No production health, final AC-033, deployment state, publication state, or RMD-06/RMD-07 readiness is inferred.
summary: PASS: all four required dimensions pass, the three original Sol findings and adverse closure finding are resolved, the preserved attempt history and final receipt are exact, and no open finding remains within this local static evidence boundary.
